name: priority-queue
description: A generic min-heap priority queue built on container/heap. The exported PriorityQueue[T] hides all heap.Interface boilerplate (Len/Less/Swap/Push/Pop) inside an unexported minHeap[T] type, exposing only New, Push(item, priority), Pop() (T, bool), and Len(). Lowest-priority items dequeue first; ties among equal priorities are broken FIFO via an insertion-order sequence counter. Standard library only, go 1.23, gofmt-clean. Verified GREEN in /tmp/wf2-go-3 with `go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./...` (plus `gofmt -l .` returning nothing).

maintain.request: Add a Peek() (T, bool) method that returns the lowest-priority item without removing it (false on an empty queue), so callers can inspect the next item before deciding whether to dequeue it. Cover it with a table/edge-case test (empty queue, no mutation after peek, repeated peek, and peek reflecting the new min after a pop).

review.bug: In minHeap.Less the tie-break comparison for equal priorities uses `seq > seq` instead of `seq < seq`, reversing insertion order so equal-priority items are dequeued LIFO instead of the documented FIFO order.
keywords: equal priorities pop lifo not fifo
